title: A Seminar on Tax Awareness for Women Entrepreneurs in Rustaq
description: As part of the Tax Authority’s ongoing efforts to build bridges of communication with the local community and simplify financial and legal concepts, the Tax Authority Department in Al Batinah South Governorate organized an awareness seminar titled The seminar was presented by Payment Analyst Nihal Al Qarni and Rahil Al Farsi. The session highlighted the rights and obligations that ensure women entrepreneurs' tax compliance, thereby supporting the growth and stability of their business ventures. Furthermore, the seminar detailed the mechanisms for calculating Income Tax and the categories subject to it, as well as how to manage Value Added Tax (VAT) in commercial activities to ensure the accuracy of accounting records. The seminar also addressed Excise Tax, the excise goods, and the health and economic dimensions that the Sultanate of Oman aims to achieve through its implementation.

title: General Tax Authority Introduces Excise Tax Warehouse Licensing Service
description: As part of its efforts to enhance the implementation of excise tax and streamline related procedures, the General Tax Authority intends to launch the excise tax warehouse licensing service. This service enables the licensing premises designated for the production, processing, possession, storage, or receipt of excise goods, whether locally produced or imported, under the tax suspension regime. This regime refers to the deferral of excise tax liability on such goods while they remain within a licensed tax warehouse, in accordance with the provisions of the Excise Tax Law and its Executive Regulations. The excise tax warehouse licensing service will be available starting from 1 April 2026 to companies producing excise goods, with plans to expand the scope of the service at a later stage to include other eligible entities. The Authority explained that the tax warehouse regime provides a flexible framework for managing excise tax, allowing the suspension of tax payment until the goods are released into the local market. It also enables the import and storage of excise goods and raw materials under a tax suspension status, thereby enhancing taxpayers’ cash flow, supporting production needs, and ensuring supply chain stability. In addition, it contributes to improved inventory management and more efficient operational planning. The tax warehouse service is considered a key enabler for supporting the business environment, as it simplifies administrative procedures related to the refund of excise tax on the import of raw materials, such as concentrates, as well as in cases involving the export of excise goods. The implementation of this service eliminates the need to submit refund applications in such cases, thereby reducing the administrative burden on registered taxpayers. The General Tax Authority affirmed that the launch of this service reflects its role of being an active partner in supporting the national economy and fostering an attractive business environment.

title: The Enhancement of Tax Awareness for Entrepreneurs
description: As part of institutional partnership to support the Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) sector, the Tax Authority department in Al Batinah South Governorate conducted a workshop titled "Tax Basics for Entrepreneurs." The session targeted participants in the "Entrepreneur Readiness Program – Establishment Phase" organized by the (SMEs) Development Authority in the governorate. Asaad Al Balushi, Senior Tax Awareness Analyst, presented a working paper introducing the tax system in the Sultanate of Oman and its recent developments. He clarified tax laws, obligations, and rights to instill a solid tax culture among entrepreneurs at the beginning of their professional careers. The working paper also reviewed aspects of the tax system, including: Principles of Income Tax and its handling mechanisms, applications of Value Added Tax (VAT) and goods subject to Excise Tax. Furthermore, the workshop raised the risks of tax evasion under the slogan "No to Tax Evasion," emphasizing the importance of voluntary compliance to strengthen the national economy.

title: Tax Awareness Workshop at Al Dhahirah Chamber
description: The Tax Authority, in collaboration with the Oman Chamber of Commerce and Industry (OCCI) branch in Al Dhahirah Governorate, organized a workshop on tax awareness and the promotion of voluntary compliance. The event was held for entrepreneurs and owners of small and medium enterprises (SMEs) across the Wilayats of Al Dhahirah Governorate. Ahmed Al Hinai, Senior Tax Awareness Analyst at the Tax Authority, showed a presentation on the Value Added Tax (VAT) law. He defined the tax imposed on all supplies of goods and services in the Sultanate of Oman, explaining the VAT mechanism, types of supplies, tax rates, and tax-exempt supplies. He also presented practical examples regarding the reverse charge mechanism and the tax due date. Later, Mazen Al Abri, a Tax Auditor at the Tax Authority, presented a working paper on the VAT registration mechanism. He clarified who is required to register in VAT and demonstrated the components of annual revenues, which include the value of supplies for registration purposes and tax groups. Additionally, Maryam Al Manthari, a Tax Auditor at the Tax Authority, presented a working paper on the mechanism of filing tax returns. In conclusion, all inquiries and questions from the attendees were addressed, covering everything related to taxes, the challenges entrepreneurs face in applying VAT and Income Tax, and how to pay taxes electronically.

title:
General Tax Authority Enables the direct application of double taxation avoidance
                                    agreements
description: As part of its ongoing efforts to develop the tax services ecosystem and enhance its effectiveness, the General Tax Authority announced the availability of the direct application of double taxation avoidance agreements service. The service aims to enable eligible entities to directly benefit from the advantages of Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements, through simplified procedures and clear standards that enhance efficiency and transparency. This service allows authorized entities to apply reduced withholding tax rate or exemptions from withholding tax when making payments to non-resident beneficiaries, in line with the provisions of the applicable tax treaties between the State of Qatar and the beneficiaries’ countries of tax residence. This contributes to faster procedures, reduced complexity, and improved tax compliance. Eligible taxpayers may apply for Trusted Entity status through the Dhareeba platform using the approved forms, in accordance with the mechanisms specified by the Authority. Eligible applicants include ministries and government entities, public authorities and institutions, financial institutions, companies listed on the Qatar Stock Exchange. The service focuses on supporting the business environment by enabling the direct application of Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements and improving the efficiency of cross-border transactions, positively impacting cash flows and strengthening investor confidence. The Authority sets the criteria for granting Trusted Entity status, which includes meeting the minimum threshold for the number or value of withholding tax transactions during the previous tax year, while retaining the right to invite government entities or eligible taxpayers to apply in accordance with approved rules and procedures. This service forms part of the Authority’s efforts to simplify tax procedures, enhance transparency and fairness, support economic diversification, and attract foreign investment—thereby contributing to strengthening the competitiveness of Qatar’s tax system and supporting financial sustainability.

title:
The General Tax Authority Announces the Implementation of the Global and Domestic
                                    Minimum Tax in Line with the Latest International Standards
description: As part of its ongoing efforts to align the national tax system with international best practices, the General Tax Authority announces the commencement of the implementation of Chapter Seven (Repealed and Re-enacted) of the Income Tax Law issued under Law No. (24) of 2018 and its amendments, which sets out the rules for applying the global and domestic minimum tax, thereby enhancing the principles of transparency and fairness in the tax system. This decision comes within the context of implementing Pillar Two of the global initiative led by the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) and the Group of Twenty (G20) to address the tax challenges arising from the digitalization of the economy, known as the Global Minimum Tax Agreement. This initiative aims to impose an effective minimum tax rate of 15% on the profits of multinational enterprises with foreign operations, provided that their revenues exceed EUR 750 million. It also represents the implementation of the latest amendment to the Income Tax Law and introduces two fundamental rules in modern tax policy: the Global Minimum Tax (Qualified Income Inclusion Rule) and the Domestic Minimum Tax (Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax). This step reflects the State of Qatar’s efforts to keep pace with international initiatives to ensure that multinational companies contribute their fair share of taxes worldwide. This decision also underscores the State’s leading role in strengthening the Inclusive Framework of the OECD and the G20 on Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS), as well as in protecting the national tax base from the unlawful shifting of profits to low-tax jurisdictions or the loss of national tax revenues to other countries. In this regard, the General Tax Authority affirmed that this approach reflects the State of Qatar’s commitment to complying with international best practices in the tax field and enhances its position as a reliable and transparent economic and financial hub on the global stage. The GTA will issue the necessary guidance manuals and executive decisions in the coming phase to clarify the implementation mechanisms, in accordance with the standards set under Pillar Two, including the Global Minimum Tax (GloBE) rules. It is noteworthy that this decision represents a strategic step in supporting the balance between attracting foreign direct investment, safeguarding tax sovereignty, and upholding financial fairness within the country.

title: Finance, Tax, and Customs Authorities Celebrate National Sports Day at Old Doha Port
description: On the occasion of National Sports Day, the Ministry of Finance, the General Tax Authority and the General Authority of Customs, organized a joint sports event at Old Doha Port. The initiative reflects the participation of government institutions in this national event, which aims to promote a culture of physical activity and encourage healthy lifestyles. The event was attended by H.E. Mr. Ali bin Ahmed Al-Kuwari, Minister of Finance; H.E. Mr. Khalifa bin Jassim Al-Jaham Al-Kuwari, President of the General Tax Authority and leaders from the General Authority of Customs, alongside a number of employees from the three authorities. Their presence underscores the importance of institutional participation in supporting national initiatives with health and community-oriented objectives. The program included a variety of sports activities, such as walking, fitness exercises, and competitive games, which allowed for broad participation and fostered interaction and communication among attendees, transforming the occasion from a symbolic celebration into active engagement in physical activity. The event’s significance lies in its contribution to a broader national approach linking health and quality of life. It highlights the role of government institutions in actively raising awareness about the importance of sports, particularly in light of modern lifestyle challenges characterized by reduced physical activity. National Sports Day is an annual occasion to renew community commitment to sports, promote positive health behaviors, and encourage individuals to adopt regular physical activity as a sustainable lifestyle, in line with the country’s vision of building a healthy, productive, and balanced society. This event was organized in implementation of the Amiri Decree issued in 2011, which designates the second Tuesday of February each year as National Sports Day, aiming to encourage sports participation and promote a healthy lifestyle.

title: State of Qatar Participates in the Sixth Meeting of the Global Forum on VAT in Paris
description: His Excellency Mr. Khalifa bin Jassim Al-Jaham Al-Kuwari, President of the General Tax Authority, headed the delegation of the State of Qatar participating in the Sixth Meeting of the Global Forum on Value Added Tax (VAT) in Paris. The forum was organized by the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) and held in Paris from 26 to 28 January 2026. The forum is considered a high-level international platform that brings together senior officials from tax administrations around the world to discuss the design and implementation of VAT/GST systems, exchange experiences in addressing challenges related to the digital economy, e-commerce, crypto-assets, and artificial intelligence, as well as to review best practices aimed at enhancing tax compliance and developing risk management mechanisms. The State of Qatar’s participation in this meeting reaffirms its commitment to strengthening international cooperation in the field of tax policy and exchanging expertise in a manner that contributes to enhancing the efficiency of domestic tax systems, in addition to supporting international efforts to modernize and develop tax frameworks in line with evolving economic and technological changes.

title: NBR conducted 1216 inspection visits in 2025 to strengthen oversight of the commercial entities
description: The National Bureau for Revenue (NBR) conducted 1216 inspection visits within the local markets in the governorates of the Kingdom of Bahrain during 2025, as part of the NBR’s continuous commitment to enhance the level of business compliance within the local markets and ensure adherence to all regulations and legislation for the effective implementation of VAT and Excise. The inspection was conducted over various sectors, such as construction, manufacturing, real estate, retail, plumbing, heat and air-conditioning installation, travel agencies, auto repair, manpower supply, jewelry trading and other sectors. This reflects the NBR’s ongoing commitment to monitoring the market, ensuring a high level of protection for consumer rights, combating VAT and Excise evasion, and spreading awareness of the rightful ways of implementation. These efforts resulted in a high compliance rate of 86.5% across local markets in 2025, reflecting the positive impact of NBR’s continuous oversight and awareness initiatives. The NBR has confirmed that effective oversight of the local market has resulted in reporting 145 VAT violations, in which administrative fines have been imposed in accordance with the Law. The most reported violations were related to the failure to comply with the conditions for issuing VAT invoices, in addition to other violations such as failure to show prices inclusive of VAT, failure to display a VAT certificate in a visible location, failure to issue VAT invoices, and issuance of VAT invoices on non-VATable goods or services. As part of NBR’s ongoing efforts to ensure proper excise implementation in the local market, the NBR reported 19 violations related to the acquisition of excise goods with unpaid excise dues, and the supply of excise goods without a valid and active digital stamp, for which administrative fines were imposed. The NBR's intensified oversight efforts also led to identification of suspicious cases of VAT and Excise evasion, in which legal actions have been taken against violators, and reported those who are proven to have committed evasion crimes which may be punishable by imprisonment for five years and a fine equivalent to three times the amount of VAT due or by imprisonment for one year and a fine equivalent to double the evaded excise. The NBR highlighted the importance of adhering to all regulations and legislation to ensure the effective implementation of VAT and Excise. The NBR also urged all concerned parties to report any violations by contacting the NBR call center at 80008001 available 24 hours, 7 days a week, or through the National Suggestions & Complaints System (Tawasul). Additionally, detailed information is available on the NBR’s website .

title: NBR announces the launch of 2026 awareness workshops and opens registration
description: The National Bureau for Revenue (NBR) announces its 2026 annual calendar of awareness workshops, as part of its ongoing efforts to raise awareness on the fundamentals of VAT and Excise and to enhance compliance, targeting key legislative and procedural aspects. The NBR stated that the upcoming series of workshops will cover a range of VAT and Excise topics, including an overview of general principles, registration procedures, VAT refund and payment mechanisms as well as compliance with VAT invoicing requirements. Specialized workshops will also highlight the implementation of VAT in specific sectors, such as construction and healthcare. These workshops will be held throughout the year, ensuring broad participation and maximum benefit while achieving the highest levels of efficiency and effectiveness. The launch of these workshops is part of the NBR’s strategy to enhance compliance by providing an interactive educational platform that offers a detailed explanation of regulatory requirements and procedures, while also providing an opportunity for participants to address their inquiries and receive immediate responses from NBR specialists during the sessions. All interested parties are invited to register for the workshops in advance through NBR’s website ( ), which will enable NBR specialists to provide workshop-related information. In addition, more information and updates will be available on NBR’s social media platforms (@Bahrainnbr), or by contacting NBR’s call center at 80008001 for any inquiries.

title: NBR: Over 27,000 entities registered for VAT by the end of 2025
description: The National Bureau for Revenue (NBR) commends the positive response from entities in complying with registration for VAT purposes. The NBR announced that the number of VAT registrants by the end of 2025 exceeded 27,070 entities. This commitment reflects the constructive collaboration of economic activity practitioners in ensuring the effective implementation of VAT in the Kingdom of Bahrain. The NBR stated that those required to register for VAT purposes are any entities with economic activities whose annual VATable sales exceeded within any of the previous twelve-months or are expected to exceed within any of the next twelve-months the mandatory registration threshold of BHD 37,500. The NBR urges all concerned to complete the VAT registration process in order to avoid administrative penalties or criminal punishments, as failure to register for VAT may be considered a case of VAT evasion as per article (63) of the VAT law, which punishments may reach imprisonment for a period of five years and a fine up to six times the amount of VAT evaded in accordance with article (64) of the VAT Law. The NBR highlights that economic activity includes any activity that is conducted in an ongoing and regular manner for the purpose of generating income, and includes commercial, industrial, agricultural, professional, services, or any use of tangible or intangible assets, and any other similar activity, for example, legal practices, licensed real estate professionals including brokers, valuers, sales agents and property managers, as well as fashion design, beauty services, events planning, auto repair, manpower suppliers and others. The NBR invites those interested in understanding the VAT registration criteria, its implementation mechanisms and ensuring compliance to join the workshop that will be conducted on Tuesday, 10 February 2026,  by filling out the available registration form on NBR website ( ). In addition, more information and updates will be available on NBR’s social media platforms (@Bahrainnbr) or by contacting NBR’s call center at 80008001 for any inquiries.

title: The Tax Authority Organizes Awareness Workshop for Social Media Influencers on Tax Compliance
description: As part of the Tax Authority's strategy aimed at strengthening cooperation with various segments of the tax community, and continuing its efforts to enhance tax awareness and culture by diversifying and expanding internal and external communication channels; it organized an awareness workshop targeting social media influencers. The workshop aimed to raise awareness regarding tax obligations and enhance compliance among advertising and promotion service providers with the tax systems and laws applicable in the Sultanate of Oman. Mahmoud Al-Rabaani, Director General of Taxpayer Services and Operations GD emphasized in his opening speech the vital role that advertising and promotion activities play in enhancing the business environment and stimulating economic activities. Following the opening speech, an intensive program was presented by Ali Al-Maimani, Director of Al Batinah South Governorate Office, and Omar Al-Washahi, Director of Large Taxpayer Audit Department. The program combined theoretical presentations with practical applications to clarify the duties and obligations associated with practicing advertising and promotion activities. Furthermore, the workshop included a comprehensive review of two main pillars: Corporate Income Tax and Value Added Tax (VAT), focusing on registration procedures, the obligations, and the legal consequences of tax evasion. The program also featured open discussion sessions during which participants were given the opportunity to ask questions and receive answers from specialists.

title: UAE approves corporate tax exemption for non-commercial sports entities
description: UAE issues Cabinet Decision No. 1 of 2026 granting tax exemptions to qualifying non-commercial sports entities under tax law
category: Politics, Ministry of Finance, UAE cabinet

title: UAE E-Invoicing Mandate: A Step-by-Step Compliance Checklist for Businesses Ahead of 2026–2027 Implementation
description: The UAE will introduce mandatory e-invoicing for businesses through a phased rollout between 2026 and 2027, requiring invoices to be issued in structured XML format and transmitted through accredited service providers on the Peppol network. The reform, led by the UAE Ministry of Finance and the Federal Tax Authority, aims to improve tax transparency, strengthen VAT compliance, and accelerate the country’s digital transformation.

title: UAE Launches Up to 50% R&D Tax Credit for Businesses
description: The UAE has officially launched Phase 1 of its Research and Development (R&D) Tax Incentives Program, introducing a non-refundable corporate tax credit of up to 50 percent on qualifying R&D expenditure capped at AED 5 million per tax period.

title: Saudi Arabia’s SEZs (Special Economic Zones): What the New Regulatory Framework Means for Foreign Investors
description: Saudi Arabia's SEZs (Special Economic Zones) are now subject to a new, comprehensive regulatory framework offering foreign investors incentives such as reduced corporate tax rates, customs exemptions, and streamlined licensing procedures. The initiative, aligned with Vision 2030, aims to attract global investment in sectors including logistics, advanced manufacturing, maritime industries, and digital technologies.
category: Economy & Trade, Saudi Arabia, Business Intelligence

title: Oman E-Invoicing and Qatar’s CbCR Tabadol Portal: The GCC’s Digital Tax Infrastructure Push
description: Oman and Qatar are advancing the GCC’s shift toward digital tax administration through new compliance platforms. Oman’s upcoming Fawtara e-invoicing system will introduce structured digital invoicing and real-time transaction monitoring, while Qatar’s Tabadol portal strengthens tax transparency by enabling multinational companies to submit Country-by-Country Reports in line with OECD BEPS standards.

title: UAE VAT Credits Expiring in 2026: What Finance Teams Need to Know
description: The 2026 UAE VAT amendments introduce a five-year limit on recovering excess input VAT, meaning businesses must review and claim historical VAT credits before they expire.

title: Oman Unveils VAT Refund Scheme for Tourists: Expected Impact on Retail and Tourism
description: Oman announced a new VAT refund scheme for tourists aiming to strengthen its retail and tourism competitiveness by aligning with regional (GCC) and global best practices.

title: Tax Treatment of Vacation Rentals in Egypt: What Property Owners and Foreign Investors Need to Know
description: Vacation rentals in Egypt are taxable when they generate recurring income, and owners must comply with income tax, VAT (in some cases), and property tax rules based on how the property is actually used. Maintaining proper records, understanding licensing and rental regulations, and using professional property management or accounting support can help owners reduce tax risks and manage their investment effectively.

title: Abu Dhabi’s Art Customs Duties Waiver Program Boosts Global Collector Access and Trade Dynamics
description: Abu Dhabi’s new Art Customs Duty Waiver Program removes import duties on qualifying artworks under a regulated framework, strengthening the emirate’s appeal to global collectors and institutions.

title: GCC, India Launch Free Trade Agreement Talks: Trade, Investment, and Sector Implications
description: The GCC and India have restarted Free Trade Agreement talks, in a move that could shape tariffs, rules of origin, customs procedures.

title: UAE Tightens Telecom Devices Import Rules: What Global Suppliers and Distributors Need to Know
description: The UAE has clarified that all telecom devices imported into the country require a TDRA customs clearance permit, reinforcing that such equipment is treated as regulated goods rather than general consumer electronics. For importers and suppliers, this underscores the need for advance compliance planning, including type approval and shipment-level permits, to avoid customs delays and market access risks.

title: Qatar Implements Global and Domestic Minimum Tax: Pillar Two Scope and Next Steps
description: Law No. 22 of 2024 amends Qatar’s Income Tax Law to implement the Qatar Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (DMTT) under OECD Pillar Two, introducing the Income Inclusion Rule and a Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax effective for fiscal years beginning on or after January 1, 2025.

title: Qatar Introduces Trusted Entity Regime Allowing Immediate Tax Treaty Benefits
description: Qatar has introduced a Trusted Entity regime, allowing approved taxpayers to apply reduced withholding tax rates or exemptions under double taxation treaties directly at source. The reform replaces the previous refund-based system, reducing administrative burdens and improving cash flow for companies making cross-border payments.

title: Türkiye Proposes 10 Percentage Crypto Income Tax and Transaction Levy on Service Providers
description: Türkiye has proposed a new crypto taxation framework introducing a 10 percent withholding tax on profits from digital asset transactions and a 0.03 percent levy on crypto service providers. The measure reflects growing regulatory oversight as the country emerges as one of the world’s largest cryptocurrency markets.
category: Tax & Accounting, Turkey / Turkiye, Business Intelligence

title: ZATCA Urges Establishments Subject to WHT to Submit Forms for March 2026
description: (ZATCA) has called on establishments subject to Withholding Tax in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to submit their withholding tax forms for last March 2026 by 10 April 2026.

title: Strengthening rule of law in international taxation: The OECD concludes second cycle of capacity-building for judges with Ukraine and looks beyond
description: The OECD concluded Phase II of the International Tax Capacity-Building Programme for Ukrainian Administrative Court Judges with its eighth virtual session. The session focused on Value Added Tax (VAT), highlighting the experience of judges from OECD countries in adjudicating VAT cases, particularly in the context of their national legislation.

title: Tax and business leaders gather at OECD to advance global dialogue on future-ready VAT systems amid digital transformation
description: The OECD Global Forum on VAT held its sixth meeting in Paris, France, on 26-28 January 2026, bringing together more than 300 representatives from 104 jurisdictions, international organisations, businesses, and other stakeholders to advance international dialogue on efficient, future-ready VAT systems in the context of digital transformation.

title: Sixth Meeting of the OECD Global Forum on VAT
description: Since 2012, the OECD Global Forum on VAT has been a unique platform for an inclusive dialogue on the design and operation of Value Added Taxes/Goods and Services Taxes (VAT/GST). This meeting will once again offer the opportunity for stakeholders from around the world to share policy analysis and experience, identify best practices and strengthen...

title: Digital Continuous Transactional Reporting for Value Added Tax: Policy and Design Considerations for Introduction and Operation
description: This report examines the design and operation aspects of digital continuous transactional reporting (DCTR) regimes for value added tax (VAT). It has been prepared in the context of a growing number of jurisdictions worldwide that have adopted or are considering the introduction of such regimes. DCTR typically requires the (near) real-time...

title: ATAF and OECD deliver new tax capacity-building workshops on transfer pricing simplification for African countries
description: The African Tax Administration Forum (ATAF) and the OECD have completed a new series of joint workshops to support advancing transfer pricing simplification across the African region, bringing together more than 130 participants from 17 African countries. Delivered in French, the workshops brought together tax officials from French-speaking...

title: ADIB mobilises AED 20.3 billion in sustainable finance in 2025
description: ABU DHABI, 6th April, 2026 (WAM) -- Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank (ADIB) has announced that it mobilised and facilitated AED 20.3 billion in sustainable finance by the end of 2025, marking a strong year of execution and reinforcing its commitment to deploy AED 60 billion in sustainable finance by 2030.This performance underscores ADIB’s role as a trusted sustainable finance partner, supporting governments, financial institutions and corporates in advancing climate action, enabling the energy transition, and delivering meaningful environmental and social impact across the UAE and the wider region. ADIB played a key role in structuring and participating in a range of landmark sustainable finance transactions across the UAE and the region, supporting clients across multiple sectors. These included large scale project financings for renewable energy developments, sustainability linked facilities for financial institutions, and debt capital market transactions within the sustainable sukuk space, serving both banks and corporates seeking Sharia compliant funding solutions. ADIB’s sustainable finance performance reflects strong market momentum alongside the Bank’s clear strategic commitment to embedding ESG principles across its financing activities. Mobilising more than AED 20 billion during the year marks a significant step toward the Bank’S AED 60 billion sustainable finance target by 2030 and reinforces ADIB’s position as a leading Islamic sustainable finance partner in the UAE and the wider region. ADIB’s sustainable finance framework continues to guide the Bank’s approach to identifying, structuring and reporting eligible green, social and sustainability-linked transactions. The Bank remains focused on supporting clients in aligning their funding strategies with national and regional sustainability agendas, while maintaining prudent risk management and disciplined execution.
